section.heading: New contracts and grants and merit-based selection procedures

subsection.heading: Congressional Policy.—
subsection.chapeau: It is the policy of Congress that—

paragraph.chapeau: an executive agency should not be required by legislation to award—

subparagraph.content: a new contract to a specific non-Federal Government entity; or
a new grant for research, development, test, or evaluation to a non-Federal Government entity; and

paragraph.content: a program, project, or technology identified in legislation be procured or awarded through merit-based selection procedures.

subsection.heading: New Contract and New Grant Described.—

paragraph.content: a contract is a new contract unless the work provided for in the contract is a continuation of the work performed by the specified entity under a prior contract; and
a grant is a new grant unless the work provided for in the grant is a continuation of the work performed by the specified entity under a prior grant.

subsection.heading: Requirements for Awarding New Contract or New Grant.—
subsection.chapeau: A provision of law may not be construed as requiring a new contract or a new grant to be awarded to a specified non-Federal Government entity unless the provision of law specifically—

paragraph.content: identifies the particular non-Federal Government entity involved; and
states that the award to that entity is required by the provision of law in contravention of the policy set forth in subsection (a).

subsection.heading: Exception.—
subsection.content: This section does not apply to a contract or grant that calls on the National Academy of Sciences to investigate, examine, or experiment on a subject of science or art of significance to an executive agency and to report on those matters to Congress or an agency of the Federal Government.
